rows.notes: Clear reading of the page year. No monetary amounts associated with this header.
Clear reading of the section title. No monetary amounts associated with this header.
Clear reading of 'Dul', which is likely an abbreviation for 'December', serving as a date header for the following entries. No monetary amounts.
Crystal clear text for both description and monetary amounts. Vertical column lines are unambiguous, and all currency values are within their valid ranges.

The description '11 16 at 5.' is clearly legible. The amount of £2 15s 0d precisely matches a calculation of '11 items at 5 shillings each' (11 * 5s = 55s = £2 15s). This suggests the '16' in the description may be an extraneous mark, an item identifier not directly tied to the monetary calculation, or a clerical error in the description. Monetary amounts and column alignment are very clear. All currency values are within their valid ranges.

Crystal clear text for both description and monetary amounts. The amount of £1 17s 6d precisely matches '15 items at 2 shillings 6 pence each' (15 * 2s 6d = 37s 6d = £1 17s 6d). Vertical column lines are unambiguous, and all currency values are within their valid ranges.

rows.description: Balance of, now carried to account
rows.notes: Crystal clear text for description and monetary amounts. Vertical column lines are unambiguous, and all currency values are within their valid ranges.

The total amount is clearly legible as £798 4s 6d. However, a sum of the preceding entry amounts (£50 0s 0d + £2 15s 0d + £1 17s 6d + £736 2s 0d + £7 15s 0d) yields £798 9s 6d. This indicates a discrepancy of 5 shillings between the sum of individual entries and the recorded total, suggesting a clerical error in the original ledger. All currency values are within their valid ranges.
